title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a tablet computer.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you buy the tablet you were looking at? || Woman: Not yet. The small one was light enough to carry, and the screen looked clear. But I read more reviews last night. [8]People say the battery only lasts a few hours, which isn't enough for travelling[/8]. || Man: That's important. You don't want it dying on the train. || Woman: I might ask someone else as well, just to be sure, but at least I have a much better idea now.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you buy the tablet you were looking at?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not yet. The small one was light enough to carry, and the screen looked clear. But I read more reviews last night. People say the battery only lasts a few hours, which isn't enough for travelling.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's important. You don't want it dying on the train.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I might ask someone else as well, just to be sure, but at least I have a much better idea now.

questions.question: Why has the woman not bought the tablet?

options.B: It is too heavy to carry.
options.C: The battery life is too short.

questions.explanation: She says reviews report that the battery only lasts a few hours, which is not enough for travelling.

extracts.context: You will hear a man telling a friend about a museum visit.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did you enjoy the science museum? || Man: Yes, especially the space section. I thought it might be too crowded because it was Saturday, but it wasn't bad. [9]The best thing was that visitors could try several experiments themselves instead of just reading about them[/9]. || Woman: That sounds much more fun than looking at displays all day. || Woman: Well, I hope it works out. It sounds as if you have thought about it carefully, which is always a sensible start.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did you enjoy the science museum?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, especially the space section. I thought it might be too crowded because it was Saturday, but it wasn't bad. The best thing was that visitors could try several experiments themselves instead of just reading about them.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds much more fun than looking at displays all day.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Well, I hope it works out. It sounds as if you have thought about it carefully, which is always a sensible start.

questions.question: What did the man like most about the museum?

options.A: Visitors could take part in activities.
options.B: It was almost empty on Saturday.
options.C: There was a large reading area.

questions.explanation: He says the best thing was that visitors could try experiments themselves.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about a local newspaper.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Do you still read the local newspaper? || Man: Sometimes. It has useful information about roadworks and local events. The sports pages are good too. [10]But I wish they would include more stories about ordinary people, not just reports from the council[/10]. || Woman: I agree. Personal stories are usually more interesting. || Woman: Maybe next time it will be simpler. Things like this often seem more difficult before you know the full story. || Woman: That is worth remembering next time too.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Do you still read the local newspaper?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Sometimes. It has useful information about roadworks and local events. The sports pages are good too. But I wish they would include more stories about ordinary people, not just reports from the council.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I agree. Personal stories are usually more interesting.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe next time it will be simpler. Things like this often seem more difficult before you know the full story.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That is worth remembering next time too.

questions.question: How could the newspaper be improved, according to the man?

options.A: It should have better sports pages.
options.B: It should include more human-interest stories.
options.C: It should give more information about roadworks.

questions.explanation: He wants more stories about ordinary people rather than only reports from the council.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a cycling event.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you still doing the charity cycling event? || Woman: Yes, although I'm a bit worried. The distance is fine because I've been training every weekend. The weather forecast looks good too. [11]I'm just nervous about cycling in a large group because I've only ever ridden with one or two friends[/11]. || Man: You'll get used to it quickly. || Woman: I agree. It is worth taking a little time over it, instead of rushing and regretting the choice later.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you still doing the charity cycling event?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, although I'm a bit worried. The distance is fine because I've been training every weekend. The weather forecast looks good too. I'm just nervous about cycling in a large group because I've only ever ridden with one or two friends.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: You'll get used to it quickly.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I agree. It is worth taking a little time over it, instead of rushing and regretting the choice later.

questions.question: What worries the woman about the cycling event?

options.C: There will be many cyclists together.

questions.explanation: She says she is nervous about cycling in a large group.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a new neighbour.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you met the new neighbour upstairs? || Man: Yes, she seems nice. She apologised because she's decorating, but I haven't heard much noise. [12]Actually, she helped me carry a heavy box from my car yesterday, which was very kind[/12]. || Woman: That's a good start. Friendly neighbours make a big difference. || Woman: That makes sense. Sometimes the practical details are more important than they seem at the beginning. || Woman: I suppose that is helpful to know.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you met the new neighbour upstairs?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, she seems nice. She apologised because she's decorating, but I haven't heard much noise. Actually, she helped me carry a heavy box from my car yesterday, which was very kind.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That's a good start. Friendly neighbours make a big difference.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That makes sense. Sometimes the practical details are more important than they seem at the beginning.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suppose that is helpful to know.

questions.question: What does the man say about his new neighbour?

options.C: She has not spoken to him yet.

questions.explanation: He says she helped him carry a heavy box, which shows she has been helpful.

extracts.context: You will hear a girl talking about a school lunch.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you like the new school lunch menu? || Girl: Some of it. The pasta was better than before, and there was more fruit. [13]But they removed the soup, which was the one thing I always bought on cold days[/13]. The sandwiches looked fine, but I can bring those from home. || Man: Maybe lots of students will ask for soup again. || Man: I can understand that. It is always useful when someone explains the situation clearly before you decide what to do.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you like the new school lunch menu?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Some of it. The pasta was better than before, and there was more fruit. But they removed the soup, which was the one thing I always bought on cold days. The sandwiches looked fine, but I can bring those from home.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Maybe lots of students will ask for soup again.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I can understand that. It is always useful when someone explains the situation clearly before you decide what to do.

questions.question: What does the girl dislike about the new lunch menu?

options.A: The pasta is worse than before.

questions.explanation: She says they removed the soup, which she always bought on cold days.
